commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a325df668c809177072f600faa412da1dc54fc32)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `model: "inherit"` handling for native `sessions_spawn`, threads requester model context into sub-agent planning, updates docs, and adds focused model-selection/spawn tests.

PR surface: Source +184, Tests +213, Docs +8. Total +405 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR adding a new model-selection mode rather than a bug report. The provided proof is targeted tests only, not a live current-main reproduction or after-fix Gateway run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review and contributor action are needed for live proof, changelog removal, and the `inherit` sentinel compatibility decision; automation cannot supply the contributor's real-environment proof.

target.title: feat(agents): inherit requester model for subagents
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `model: "inherit"` support for native sub-agent spawns so child runs can follow the requester session's active model.
- Threads the requester model context from tool creation through `sessions_spawn` into direct sub-agent planning, with a persisted-session fallback when needed.
- Documents the new sub-agent model inheritance behavior and config precedence.
- Out of scope: ACP runtime model inheritance; ACP spawns keep their existing model handling.

Review focus: model-selection precedence, the missing-requester-model error path, and whether docs describe the new behavior clearly.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No formal maintainer request; this addresses a configuration gap where sessions can use different active models but derived sub-agents could not intentionally follow the requester model.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: native `sessions_spawn` can now use `model: "inherit"` or sub-agent model config set to `"inherit"` to resolve the child model from the requester session.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS with the repo test wrapper.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: test output reported 2 Vitest shards passed; 181 tests passed total.
- Observed result after fix: inheritance resolves to the requester model, missing requester model returns a clear error, and session spawn tool context passes the active model provider/id into native sub-agent spawn.
- What was not tested: a live end-to-end Gateway chat spawn.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: validation is focused on unit/seam coverage. `node scripts/check-changed.mjs` was started and passed conflict-marker, changelog, guard, dependency pin/patch, and core production typecheck lanes, but was stopped after `tsgo:core:test` produced no output for over 15 minutes.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing tests covered concrete model overrides and config defaults but had no requester-model inheritance path.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/gateway/config-agents.md docs/tools/subagents.md src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn-plan.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.runtime.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test-helpers.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts src/agents/tool-description-presets.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/types.agents.ts`
- `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-changed.mjs` (partial; stopped during `tsgo:core:test` after 969s without output)

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Model-selection tests for explicit `inherit`, configured `inherit`, alias preservation, and missing requester model behavior.
- `sessions_spawn` tool tests to verify active model provider/id is passed into native sub-agent context.
- Direct sub-agent spawn tests to verify requester model context is persisted on the child session.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Sub-agent spawns could select explicit or configured concrete models, but there was no supported way to make a child follow the requester session's active model.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `agents.defaults.subagents.model` and `agents.list[].subagents.model` now accept `"inherit"` as a special value.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model resolution precedence for native sub-agent spawns.

How is that risk mitigated?

The implementation keeps existing concrete model precedence, resolves `"inherit"` only for sub-agent model candidates, errors when inheritance is requested without a requester model, and adds focused tests at the model-selection, tool, and direct-spawn seams.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and any maintainer-requested live Gateway proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet.
target.author: gausons

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
